description: Bot 5 levels: per-level buffer (IBH=0.75pt, others=1.0pt) — incremental refinement on top of C

changes_from_previous.ibh_buffer_change: BOT_ENTRY_LIMIT_BUFFER_PTS converted from float 1.0 to dict {'IBH': 0.75}. Other levels fall back to 1.0pt default. Mechanically motivated: IBH is the smallest target (T6/S20), so 1pt of slippage eats 17% of target; tighter buffer keeps wins closer to target_pts. Buffer sweep showed IBH's $/trade declines monotonically as buffer widens — only level with that signal.
changes_from_previous.rationale: +$0.56/day P&L, -$16 MaxDD vs C in real-sim. Modest but real. IBH=0.5pt was worse (-$0.04/day, +$102 MaxDD) — too tight, loses volume. 0.75 is the sweet spot.

infrastructure_changes.bot_trader_dict_support: Modified bot_trader.py:443+ to accept BOT_ENTRY_LIMIT_BUFFER_PTS as either float (current) or dict (per-level, with 1.0 fallback). Backwards compatible — float still works.

IBH_05_others_1.note: Too tight — loses volume. NOT DEPLOYED.

experiments: per_level_buffer_sweep (post-process): identified IBH as the only level with monotonic decline in $/tr as buffer widens — mechanical signal (T6 target absorbs less slippage)
run_ibh_buffer_test (real-sim): C baseline + IBH=0.75 + IBH=0.5. IBH=0.75 wins both axes, IBH=0.5 fails on volume.
key_insights: Per-level buffer customization is justified ONLY when there's a mechanical reason — small target / slippage ratio for IBH. Other levels (T10, T12) showed flat curves across buffers; tuning them would be overfitting.

Real-sim matched post-process closely on this finding (post-process predicted +$0.69/day, real-sim +$0.56). Earlier today, post-process was wildly wrong on cap-opt and buffer-1 vs target/2. The difference: this finding is mechanically grounded; those weren't.
IBH=0.5 vs IBH=0.75 illustrates the curve: tighter buffer helps quality up to a point, then loses volume faster than it adds quality. Don't extrapolate past the optimum.
Quarterly decay caveat: full-sample +$0.56/day full-sample is likely +$0.20/day in Q4-like recent regime. Still positive, just smaller. The MaxDD reduction is more concrete.
